seoTitle: Enter a Refund From a Vendor in QuickBooks
metaDescription: Record a vendor refund in QuickBooks Online correctly using a Vendor Credit plus a matching Bank Deposit to keep your ledger balanced.
intro: A refund from a supplier needs a two-part entry in QuickBooks Online to stay balanced: a Vendor Credit and a matching Bank Deposit. This guide walks through both steps so a returned payment lands correctly in your books without throwing off your expense history.

steps.heading: Create the vendor credit
steps.body: Click + New, select Vendor Credit, and choose the supplier returning the funds.

steps.heading: Map the credit to the original expense
steps.body: Select the same expense account that was used for the original purchase and enter the refund amount.

steps.heading: Save the credit
steps.body: Add a memo note explaining the refund, then click Save and Close.

steps.heading: Record the bank deposit
steps.body: Click + New again, select Bank Deposit, choose the checking account, and add the vendor under Received From.

steps.heading: Link and verify
steps.body: Select Vendor Credit or Accounts Payable in the account field, enter the matching amount, save, and confirm the entry matches your live bank feed.

faq.question: Why does a vendor refund need two separate entries?
faq.answer: A Vendor Credit offsets your original expense, while a Bank Deposit records the cash actually returning to your account; together they keep both sub-ledgers accurate.

faq.question: What account should I use on the bank deposit?
faq.answer: Select Vendor Credit Card or Accounts Payable in the Account field so the deposit properly links back to the credit you created.

faq.question: Will this update my vendor's balance?
faq.answer: Yes, pairing the credit with the deposit clears the appropriate amount from that vendor's outstanding balance.

faq.question: How do I confirm the refund posted correctly?
faq.answer: Check your Bank Transactions feed to see that the inbound deposit matches your recorded entry.
